text.title: Wrap variable-cardinality extraction in {items: [...]}
text.summary: Never expose a bare array as the top-level structured-output type; wrap with items plus cardinality metadata.

section.title: The rule
section.p: For any extraction with variable N, the top-level schema is an object containing items: list[Entity] plus total_found and truncated metadata. The bare list[Entity] return shape is forbidden because (a) strict mode requires top-level objects, (b) downstream code has to special-case zero/one/many.

rule.statement: For variable-cardinality extraction, declare the top-level schema as an object with at least items: list[Entity], total_found: int, and truncated: bool. Never return a bare list as the top-level structured-output type.
rule.rationale: OpenAI/Azure strict mode rejects top-level arrays because additionalProperties only applies to objects. On other providers, list[Entity] collapses zero/one/many into inconsistent shapes ([], null, single dict, [ent], ent). The wrapper standardises the shape and exposes hooks for count and truncation diagnostics.

example.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
class Entity(BaseModel): name: str kind: str span_start: int span_end: int
class EntityList(BaseModel): model_config = {"extra": "forbid"} total_found: int = Field(description="Count of entities; 0 if none.") truncated: bool = Field(description="True if more entities exist beyond the limit.") items: list[Entity]
example.note: One stable shape for zero, one, or many. total_found lets the consumer detect "no entities" without inspecting len(items); truncated flags incomplete extraction.

example.code:
# Bare list — fails strict mode and ambiguous for zero/one cases. EntityList = list[Entity]
# Or worse: optional list with no metadata. class EntityListBad(BaseModel): items: list[Entity] | None = None
example.note: Strict-mode endpoints reject the top-level array. Optional-list shape forces every consumer to write `if items is None or len(items) == 0`.

antipattern.description: Asking for "extract all entities" and accepting the model's reply as either a dict (single entity) or a list (many entities).
antipattern.reason: The same prompt yields different top-level shapes depending on how many entities the model thinks it found. Production code ends up branching on `isinstance(result, list)` everywhere — a known source of crashes when an empty result returns null or {}.

section.reference: Working batch-entity-extraction schema with items + total_found + truncated.
Simon Willison — llm `--schema-multi` pattern (the wrapper formalised in a CLI).
Databricks — end-to-end structured extraction with LLM, batch entity wrapper.
